chapters.title: SECTION 01: WELCOME TO AMIOSH & OUR IDENTITY
chapters.bodyHtml:
1.1 Welcome Message

We are pleased to welcome you to AMIOSH. Our organization is built on a simple,

uncompromising principle: Performance, Accountability, and Discipline drive success.

At AMIOSH, every single role contributes directly to our business, our corporate

reputation, and our customers. Whether you operate in Sales & Operations,

Management, or Information Technology (IT), your daily work directly impacts our

ability to deliver premier safety solutions, sustain corporate growth, and maintain strict

client trust.

As part of this journey, we expect every employee to:

• Take absolute ownership of their responsibilities.

• Deliver measurable, impact-driven results.

• Maintain unwavering professionalism at all times.

• Continuously improve their industry skills and operational performance.

1.2 Our Core Vision

"Our commitment toward fostering a comprehensive safety ecosystem by becoming

the best fully integrated organization in Occupational Safety and Health."

1.3 Quality & Safety Standards Alignment

In alignment with our corporate objective to lead the OSH sector, all employees must

actively uphold and practice our internal systems. Our operational workflows are

mapped to international quality frameworks, ensuring that every training syllabus,

safety audit report, and corporate advisory we issue meets the highest benchmark of

industrial safety and regulatory compliance.

chapters.title: SECTION 02: TEAM MEMBER CLASSIFICATION & ONBOARDING
chapters.bodyHtml:
2.1 Employment Categories

All personnel engaged by AMIOSH shall fall strictly under one of the following

classifications, as specified in their respective Letters of Appointment:

• Permanent Employees: Team members who have successfully completed

their probationary period and have been formally confirmed in writing for

ongoing service.

• Probationary Employees: Newly hired team members undergoing an initial

trial phase to assess suitability for permanent absorption.

• Fixed-Term / Project-Based Contract Employees (Bona Fide): Team

members hired for a defined duration or whose employment is strictly tied to a

specific client purchase order, project code, or master service agreement.

• Temporary Employees: Personnel engaged on a short-term basis for

administrative or seasonal spikes.

• Interns: Students undergoing industrial training. Interns are governed by an

educational training agreement and are not entitled to standard employee

benefits except for an assigned monthly allowance.

2.2 Probation Period, Evaluation, and Confirmation

The standard probationary period for all new hires is three (3) to six (6) months. This

period is a trial phase for mutual evaluation.

• Extension: The Company reserves the absolute right to extend the probationary

period for up to an additional three (3) months if performance metrics or cultural

alignment are deemed insufficient.

• Non-Automatic Confirmation: Confirmation of employment is strictly not

automatic. It must be issued via a formal written Letter of Confirmation from the

Human Resources Department.

• Limitation of Benefits: Probationary employees are eligible for basic statutory

leave but are excluded from premium corporate benefits, such as the monthly

company medical card subsidy.

chapters.title: SECTION 03: OFFICE OPERATIONS & WORK CODES
chapters.bodyHtml:
3.1 Standard Office Hours

AMIOSH operates on a standard corporate hour structure designed to maximize

operational excellence while supporting employee well-being and work-life integration.

• Standard Working Hours: Monday to Friday, from 8:30 AM to 5:30 PM.

• Rest Periods: A designated 1-hour lunch break is allocated daily from 1:00 PM

to 2:00 PM (except on Fridays, where rest hours are adjusted to accommodate

religious observances).

3.2 Attendance Tracking & Digital Logging (Dual-System Entry)

Accurate timekeeping is critical for operational metrics, payroll verification, and safety

compliance. AMIOSH strictly enforces a dual-system attendance tracking framework:

• Office-Based Staff & Interns (Daily Entry/Exit): Must record their daily

attendance at arrival (no later than 8:30 AM) and departure (no earlier than 5:30

PM) using the corporate physical thumbprint scanner stationed at the office

premises.

• Site Teams: Must log their daily attendance and deployment locations using the

designated mobile tracking applications or digital tools provided within the

company ecosystem (Kijo system).

• HR Management Integration: The Kijo system is utilized exclusively for

processing formal employee Leave, Medical Certificates (MC), and Claims

management. The physical thumbprint data remains the primary source of truth

for daily physical office attendance audit logs.

3.3 Unexcused Absences & Job Abandonment (EA 1955 Sec 15(2))

• No-Show Classification: Any employee who is absent from work without

obtaining prior approved leave or without notifying their immediate supervisor

within forty-eight (48) hours will be classified as an unexcused absence.

• Job Abandonment: In strict accordance with Section 15(2) of the Malaysian

Employment Act 1955, an employee who is absent from work for more than two

(2) consecutive working days without prior leave or a reasonable, verified

excuse communicated to Management shall be deemed to have broken their

contract of service. This results in disciplinary action up to summary dismissal.

3.4 Professional Conduct at Client Sites

As an OSH organization, our personnel represent safety, discipline, and authority.

When deployed to client sites (such as manufacturing plants, factories, or cement

mills), employees must strictly adhere to both AMIOSH policies and the client's internal

site safety rules. Any behaviour that compromises site safety, displays

unprofessionalism, or tarnishes AMIOSH's corporate standing will face immediate

severe disciplinary action.

3.5 Business Interruption & Force Majeure Arrangements

In the event of unforeseen operational or business interruptions caused by factors

beyond the company’s reasonable control (e.g., natural disasters, flash floods,

pandemics, infrastructure failures, or government-enforced corporate or factory

closures), Management reserves the absolute right to implement temporary alternative

operational protocols. This includes enforcing Mandatory Work-From-Home (WFH)

regimes, temporary adjustments to shift duties, or requiring employees to utilize

accumulated Annual Leave entitlements in strict accordance with prevailing regulatory

and statutory guidelines.

chapters.title: SECTION 04: ANTI-HARASSMENT & WORKPLACE RESPECT
chapters.bodyHtml:
4.1 Zero-Tolerance Policy for Sexual Harassment

In strict compliance with Part XVA of the Employment Act 1955, AMIOSH maintains a

professional, safe, and respectful workplace entirely free from discrimination and

harassment. The Company enforces a zero-tolerance policy against any act of sexual

harassment. Sexual harassment is defined as any unwanted conduct of a sexual

nature—whether verbal, non-verbal, visual, psychological, or physical—which causes

offence, humiliation, or threatens an individual's well-being.

4.2 Reporting Mechanism & Grievance Procedures

Any employee who feels they are a victim of sexual harassment, or who witnesses

such behaviour, has the right and responsibility to lodge a formal complaint

immediately in writing to the HR Department or directly to Senior Management. All

reports and subsequent investigations will be treated with absolute confidentiality.

4.3 Inquiry & Disciplinary Action

If a preliminary review shows merit, the accused employee may be suspended under

statutory guidelines pending a formal internal inquiry. If confirmed, it will be classified

as a Major Offence leading to summary dismissal.

4.4 Psychosocial Safety & Mental Well-being (OSHA 2022 Alignment)

In strict compliance with the occupational duties governed under the Occupational

Safety and Health (Amendment) Act 2022, AMIOSH is dedicated to maintaining a

workspace free from psychosocial hazards. Every employee has a reciprocal duty to

refrain from engaging in toxic workspace behavior, systemic workplace bullying,

professional sabotage, or intentional intimidation tactics that deliberately undermine

the mental health, psychological well-being, or operational stability of fellow team

members. Violations of psychosocial safety codes shall be met with strict disciplinary

corrective measures.

chapters.title: SECTION 05: CORPORATE APPEARANCE, DRESS CODE & UNIFORMS
chapters.bodyHtml:
5.1 General Business Attire Standards

Employees must maintain a clean, neat, and professional appearance at all times.

Office-based staff must adhere to a Smart Casual or Formal Corporate dress code.

5.2 Official AMIOSH Corporate Shirt & Uniform Policy

To project a unified, elite corporate identity, the company provides official AMIOSH

uniform shirts. Wearing the official AMIOSH uniform is mandatory on designated

corporate days, during all client programs, DOSH/NIOSH presentations, corporate

training courses, and public field operations.

chapters.title: SECTION 06: COMPANY ASSET & TECHNOLOGY MANAGEMENT
chapters.bodyHtml:
6.1 Custody & Proper Use of Company Assets

Assets issued remain the exclusive, permanent property of AMIOSH and are provided

strictly for official company operations.

6.2 Software Security, Licenses & Illegal Downloads

Employees are strictly forbidden from downloading, installing, or executing

unauthorized, unlicensed, or pirated software, torrent files, or unapproved applications

onto company devices.

6.3 Weekly Cloud Data Backup Mandate

All consultants, trainers, and administrative staff are required to back up all active

project files, training slides, customer correspondence, and safety reports weekly into

the official AMIOSH cloud storage system.

6.4 Damage, Loss, and Carelessness Liability

Any damage, malfunction, or loss of a company asset must be reported within seventy-

two (72) hours. If an internal investigation proves that the loss or destruction of an

asset was the direct result of gross personal carelessness, negligence, or a deliberate

breach of this policy, AMIOSH reserves the absolute right to recover the repair or

replacement costs as permitted by law, subject to the employee's explicit written

consent in this Handbook.

6.5 Unauthorized Audio, Video, and Digital Recording Policy

• Strict Prohibition: Employees are strictly prohibited from making any audio,

video, digital, or photographic recordings of any internal company discussions,

operations, weekly meetings, monthly meetings, strategic sessions,

performance reviews, or human resource conversations. This prohibition

applies regardless of whether the meeting is conducted in-person or via digital

communication platforms (such as Microsoft Teams, Zoom, or WhatsApp calls),

and applies to any reason or purpose whatsoever.

• Exclusive Management Authority: The authority to record, log, transcribe, or

formally minute any company meeting or discussion is reserved exclusively for

the Human Resources (HR) Department and/or designated members of

Management for official corporate documentation and verification purposes.

• Classification of Breach: Any unauthorized recording, attempted recording,

possession, or dissemination of such recorded material constitutes a severe

breach of corporate confidentiality, internal data security, and fiduciary trust. A

violation of this policy shall be classified as a Major Offence, rendering the

employee liable to immediate progressive disciplinary action up to and including

summary dismissal.

6.6 Acceptable Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) Tools & Data Privacy Boundaries

To ensure complete structural data compliance under the Personal Data Protection

Act (PDPA) 2010, employees are strictly prohibited from uploading, inputting,

transferring, or processing any confidential company data, corporate trade secrets,

source codes, proprietary customer registries, or personal records into open-sourced

or public Artificial Intelligence engines (including but not limited to ChatGPT, Gemini,

or Claude) without explicit, written authorization from the IT and HR Department. Any

corporate data spill or proprietary breach resulting from unauthorized AI employment

shall be treated as an uncompromised material breach of trust.

chapters.title: SECTION 07: LEAVE ENTITLEMENTS
chapters.bodyHtml:
7.1 Annual Leave (AL) Allocation & Accrual Tiers (EA 1955 Compliant)

Paid annual leave entitlements are strictly bound to the continuous length of corporate

service in absolute compliance with Section 60E of the Employment Act 1955:

• Tier A (Less than 2 years of continuous service): 8 working days per full

calendar year.

• Tier B (2 years but less than 5 years of continuous service): 12 working days

per full calendar year.

• Tier C (5 years and above of continuous service): 16 working days per full

calendar year.

All applications for formal annual leave must be filed digitally through the Kijo system

at least three (3) working days in advance and require express approval from line

management.

7.2 Zero Accumulation & Yearly Forfeiture Policy

AMIOSH enforces a strict Zero Accumulation Policy for leave management. All annual

leave allocated for a specific calendar year must be utilized within that year. Any

unutilized annual leave balance standing on 31st December will be automatically

forfeited. There shall be no roll-over of leave to the following year, nor any encashment

of leave in lieu.

The approval to carry forward leave is strictly at the absolute discretion of

Management, based solely on critical operational requirements where the employee

was explicitly requested by the Company to defer their leave. Subject to such formal

management approval, an employee may be permitted to carry forward a maximum

of five (5) days of unutilized annual leave to the next calendar year, which must be

fully utilized within the first two (2) months of the new year, failing which it will be strictly

forfeited. Employees cannot claim carry-forward leave as an automatic right.

7.3 Paid Outpatient Sick Leave & Hospitalization (EA 1955 Sec 60F Compliant)

Paid non-hospitalization outpatient sick leave is allocated per calendar year in strict

conformity with Section 60F of the Employment Act 1955:

• Less than 2 years of continuous service: 14 working days per calendar year.

• 2 years but less than 5 years of continuous service: 18 working days per

calendar year.

• 5 years and above of continuous service: 22 working days per calendar year.

• Hospitalization Leave: A fixed allocation of sixty (60) days per calendar year is

provided if formal ward admission or inpatient clinical containment is certified

necessary, completely independent of the outpatient sick leave balances

above.

7.4 Mandatory MC Notification Windows

Official Medical Certificates (MC) must be digitally uploaded onto the Kijo system

immediately upon returning to work and supervisor notified within 48 hours.

7.5 Statutory Parental Leave Tiers

• Maternity Leave: Eligible female employees are entitled to 98 consecutive days

of paid maternity leave.

• Paternity Leave: Confirmed male employees are entitled to 7 consecutive days

of paid paternity leave per confinement (up to 5 confinements).

7.6 Non-Statutory Compassionate, Matrimonial & Religious Leave

• Compassionate Leave: Confirmed employees are eligible for up to three (3)

days of paid compassionate leave for immediate family.

• Matrimonial Leave: Confirmed employees are granted three (3) days of paid

leave for their first legal marriage.

• Umrah / Haji Leave: Confirmed Muslim employees are eligible for a one-time

clean entitlement of up to seven (7) consecutive days of paid unrecorded leave

to perform the Umrah or Haji pilgrimage throughout their tenure with AMIOSH.

Application must be submitted through the Kijo system at least thirty (30) days

in advance, supported by official travel itineraries and registration documents

from the licensed travel agency.

chapters.title: SECTION 08: COMPENSATION, BENEFITS & CLAIMS MANAGEMENT
chapters.bodyHtml:
8.1 Salary Administration, Pay Cycles & Cut-Off Dates

Salaries are processed and credited directly into employees' designated bank

accounts on the 26th day of each calendar month. Statutory deductions (EPF,

SOCSO, EIS) are fully detailed on pay slips.

8.2 Outpatient Medical Claim Reimbursement

Confirmed permanent employees are entitled to an outpatient medical reimbursement

benefit of up to RM 300.00 per calendar year on a "pay first, then claim" basis via the

Kijo system.

8.3 Corporate Group Insurance & Medical Cards

Confirmed permanent employees are enrolled in the company’s Group Medical Card

scheme. AMIOSH funds this benefit up to a maximum contribution of RM 58.25 per

month per eligible staff member.

• Claw-Back Provision & Written Consent: If an employee resigns or their

contract is terminated after the company has paid the annual premium

subscription for the current calendar year, AMIOSH reserves the right to

recover the unutilized, pro-rated premium amount. The employee hereby

expressly provides prior written consent under Section 24 of the Employment

Act 1955 for the recovery of this pro-rated debt directly from their final salary

settlement.

8.4 Petty Cash, Corporate Cards & General Expense Guidelines

• Petty Cash Limit: Minor, emergency office expenses valued at RM 100.00 and

below require an original, machine-printed official receipt or tax invoice.

• GLYD Card & Credit Card: Employees issued with a corporate GLYD card or

company Credit Card are strictly personally liable for ensuring all transactions

are for approved business expenses only. A complete reconciliation report,

along with original machine-printed tax invoices, must be submitted via the

finance system within seven (7) days of the transaction. Any unauthorized or

undocumented spending will be recovered directly via salary deduction based

on the written consent executed within this Handbook.

chapters.title: SECTION 09: OSH FIELD OPERATIONS: ALLOWANCES & TRAVEL
chapters.bodyHtml:
9.1 Outstation Deployment & Field Operations Allowances

(A) Overnight Outstation Allowance (200km or More & Overnight Stay):

Employees deployed for field consultations, corporate training, or safety audits that

require traveling a distance of 200 kilometres or more from AMIOSH HQ and require

an overnight stay are eligible for the daily outstation allowance:

• Interns: RM 30.00 per night

• Less than 2 years of continuous service: RM 50.00 per night

• 2 years but less than 5 years of continuous service: RM 80.00 per night

• 5 years and above of continuous service: RM 100.00 per night

(B) Late-Return Day-Trip Allowance (200km or More, No Overnight Stay but Late

Return):

Employees deployed for site assignments that require traveling a distance of 200

kilometres or more from AMIOSH HQ who return on the same day (Day-Trip) without

an overnight stay, but are required to travel or work late into the night, are entitled to

a flat Late-Return Allowance of RM 100.00, provided the employee arrives back at HQ

or their residence after 10:00 PM and the deployment is verified by HR. (Toll and

parking remain claimable at actual cost).

9.2 Weekend & Public Holiday Specialized Training/Audit Allowance

Employees executing specialized training or audit duties on rest days, weekends, or

public holidays are eligible for a daily allowance based on their category. All claims

are subject to prior approval from the Human Resources (HR) Department.

• Less than 2 years of continuous service: RM 80.00 (Saturday/Sunday) / RM

130.00 (Public Holidays)

• 2 years but less than 5 years of continuous service (Saturday/Sunday) / RM

160.00 (Public Holidays)

• 5 years and above of continuous service (Saturday/Sunday) / RM 230.00

(Public Holidays)

9.3 Mileage Reimbursement Rates

• Motorcars: RM 0.60 per kilometer.

• Motorcycles: RM 0.30 per kilometer.

chapters.title: SECTION 10: INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY & MARKET PROTECTION
chapters.bodyHtml:
10.1 Exclusive Intellectual Property (IP) Ownership

All training modules, safety presentation slides, audit checklists, proprietary software

architectures, source codes, and operational templates developed constitute the

exclusive intellectual property of the Company.

10.2 Maintenance of Active Professional Accreditations

Consultants and trainers whose roles require professional certifications (e.g., DOSH,

NIOSH, CIDB registrations) are personally responsible for maintaining their active

status.

10.3 Confidentiality, Proprietary Trade Secrets & Non-Solicitation

To protect AMIOSH's confidential client blueprints, databases, and proprietary

workflows, employees are strictly prohibited from utilizing company trade secrets for

external trading. For a period of twelve (12) months following the termination of

employment for any reason, a former employee shall not directly or indirectly exploit

AMIOSH’s active proprietary account structures or solicit corporate safety business

from any active client, account, or partner of AMIOSH. Any breach targeting the

company's established commercial assets will result in immediate legal action for

damages.

10.4 Non-Compete Post-Employment Restrictions

Upon separation of employment from AMIOSH for any reason whatsoever, the former

employee shall not, for a strict restrictive duration of twelve (12) calendar months,

engage in, operate, establish, or accept active corporate employment with any direct

commercial business competitor of AMIOSH within a structural territorial radius of fifty

(50) kilometers from AMIOSH Headquarters. This restriction explicitly binds the former

employee from providing identical or substantially similar occupational safety and

health (OSH) public training, specialized corporate safety consultancy, or statutory

third-party audit executions.

chapters.title: SECTION 11: PROGRESSIVE DISCIPLINE & OFFENCES
chapters.bodyHtml:
11.1 Progressive Disciplinary Framework

• Stage 1: Verbal Warning & Written Record Note.

• Stage 2: First Official Written Warning Letter.

• Stage 3: Second & Final Written Warning Letter.

• Stage 4: Show Cause & Domestic Inquiry (DI) in line with Section 14 of the

Employment Act 1955.

11.2 Categorization of Offences

(A) MINOR OFFENCES

Administrative or procedural non-compliance that impacts operational efficiency.

Typically met with Stage 1 or Stage 2 progressive actions.

1. Casual unexcused tardiness or late arrival to work (entering past 8:30 AM)

without an authorized operational cause.

2. Departing the corporate office premises prior to designated closing hours

(before 5:30 PM) without prior supervisor clearance.

3. Failure to display neat corporate grooming or comply with mandatory AMIOSH

corporate uniform protocols on designated field, training, or executive

presentation days.

4. Failure or neglect to log out of structural office assets or leaving allocated

workspaces and common training environments in disorganized, unkempt

states.

5. Passive non-responsiveness or protracted delay in acknowledging urgent,

official corporate group communications or operational project group platforms

during formal working hours.

(B) SERIOUS OFFENCES

Operational negligence or data security omissions that directly degrade service quality

or risk institutional structure. Subject to Stage 3 final reprimands or temporary

performance suspensions.

1. Insubordination: Wilful non-compliance, defiance, or blunt refusal to carry out

lawful, rational operational instructions issued by immediate supervisors or

Management.

2. System Data Negligence: Blatant failure to adhere to the Mandatory Weekly

Cloud Data Backup protocol into designated AMIOSH cloud storage clusters

under Section 6.3.

3. MC Timeline Infraction: Failure to upload verified Medical Certificates (MC) onto

the Kijo system or failure to explicitly alert line managers within forty-eight (48)

hours of clinical absence under Section 7.4.

4. Negligent AI Utilization: Implementing open AI generation tools to execute

corporate advisories or compliance drafting without professional cross-

verification, resulting in inaccurate structural output or corporate

misrepresentation.

5. System Identity Laxity: Sharing, leaking, lending, or exposing personalized Kijo

system access credentials, user tokens, or operational passwords to other

coworkers or unverified external parties.

6. Thumbprint Scanner Non-Compliance: Intentionally failing to record primary

daily entry or exit timestamps via the physical thumbprint scanner while being

physically present in the office, and resorting to manual administrative overrides

or digital excuses without verifiable technical malfunction of the physical

machine.

7. Unprofessional On-Site Posture: Exhibiting hostile behavior, visible

discourtesy, or failure to observe on-site safety codes while deployed to client

industrial environments or corporate facilities (Section 3.4).

(C) MAJOR OFFENCES

Fundamental breaches of employment contract, statutory boundaries, or corporate

governance. Deemed absolute grounds for summary dismissal under Section 14(1) of

the Employment Act 1955 without notice.

1. Job Abandonment: Absolute absence from work for more than two (2)

consecutive working days without prior approved leave or verified,

communicated emergency grounds under Section 15(2) of the EA 1955.

2. Breach of Absolute Confidentiality: Willfully executing any unauthorized audio,

video, photograph, or digital screen-capture recording of any internal corporate

discussions, operational assemblies, performance evaluations, or human

resource reviews (physical or online platforms) under Section 6.5.

3. Data Spill & Privacy Violation: Exporting proprietary client registries,

commercial databases, corporate trade secrets, or salary structures into

unauthorized public computing tools or public AI nodes, violating the core

Security Principle of the PDPA 2010.

4. Corporate Database Manipulation: Deliberately manipulating, falsifying,

backdating, or altering core system audit logs, physical thumbprint databases,

or approved leave/MC tracking registries within the Kijo ecosystem without

explicit written authorization from the HR Director.

5. Fraud and Deception: Falsifying official reimbursement profiles, traveling claims

(e-Claims), vendor receipts, project milestone declarations, or submitting

forged Medical Certificates (MC).

6. Theft and Financial Malfeasance: Unauthorized extraction of company

property, misappropriating petty cash funds, or demanding/accepting illicit

kickbacks, bribes, or private considerations from any client or third-party

vendor.

7. Statutory Sexual Harassment: Engaging in any unwelcome physical, verbal,

non-verbal, visual, or psychological conduct of a sexual nature that demeans,

threatens, or degrades any employee within the corporate workspace under

Part XVA of the EA 1955.

8. Moonlighting & Competing Trade: Directly engaging in secondary employment,

private OSH consultancy ventures, parallel contract commissions, or

unauthorized external corporate representation without the formal written

authorization of the Managing Director.

11.3 Exclusive Service & Non-Moonlighting Policy

To maintain absolute focus, objectivity, and corporate integrity, all employees are

strictly prohibited from engaging in any secondary business, external consultations,

freelance OSH advisory, or secondary employment, whether paid or unpaid, without

explicit prior written authorization from the Managing Director. Engaging in

unauthorized secondary employment is classified as a Major Offence and constitutes

grounds for summary dismissal.

11.4 Employees Facing Court Proceedings & Criminal Charges

• (A) Mandatory Notification Window: Any employee who is investigated,

arrested, charged, or facing any active prosecution or criminal proceedings in

a Court of Law (including Shariah Criminal or Civil Criminal Courts) must notify

the Human Resources (HR) Department in writing within forty-eight (48) hours

of such incident. Failure to report constitutes a Major Offence.

• (B) Suspension Pending Court Trial: In line with corporate governance, if an

employee is charged with a criminal offence that directly or indirectly impacts

the company’s reputation, fiduciary trust, or operational integrity, Management

reserves the absolute right to suspend the employee from active duty. The

suspension terms and salary adjustments during this period shall be governed

under Section 14(2) of the Employment Act 1955.

• (C) Impact on Operational Leave: For civil or personal court cases (e.g.,

bankruptcy, divorce, or civil lawsuits) where the employee is required to attend

court physical/online hearings, the employee must utilize their verified Annual

Leave (AL) or apply for Unpaid Leave (UL) subject to operational approval.

Court attendance cannot be claimed as an automatic right or paid unrecorded

leave.

• (D) Summary Dismissal upon Conviction: If an employee is found guilty and

convicted of any criminal offence by a Court of Law, it shall be classified as a

Major Offence under Section 11.2, resulting in immediate summary dismissal

without notice or salary in lieu.

chapters.title: SECTION 12: RESIGNATION, EXIT CLEARANCE & LEGAL SAFEGUARDS
chapters.bodyHtml:
12.1 Resignation Notice Periods

An employee who wishes to resign must provide formal written notice or pay salary in

lieu of notice. The minimum notice periods are structured strictly as follows to ensure

business continuity:

• Probationary Employees: One (1) month written notice.

• Confirmed Personnel (Continuous service BELOW five (5) years): One (1)

month written notice.

• Confirmed Personnel (Continuous service of five (5) years and ABOVE): Two

(2) months written notice.

12.2 Offboarding Asset Clearance Timelines

Upon submitting a resignation or receiving a notice of termination, all issued company

assets—laptops, specialized testing equipment, software tokens, and access cards—

must be returned in pristine operational condition to the IT and Operations Department

before their final working day.

12.3 Debt Recovery Mutual Consent Agreement

In accordance with Section 24 of the Employment Act 1955, the employee hereby

explicitly provides prior and unconditional written consent that any unreturned

company asset value, outstanding company advanced funds, unauthorized expense

balances, or pro-rated clawback items shall be treated as a lawful debt due to

AMIOSH. The Company is legally authorized to deduct and set off such certified debts

directly from the employee’s final month’s salary, final payments, or any monetary

settlement due to the employee upon separation.
